mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate directly raises the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction during rate hike periods, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) across 50+ observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.5%, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces aggregate consumer spending power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales growth and CPI inflation indicates demand-pull price pressures that force monetary tightening expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals strengthening economic output that boosts corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in > 50% of months with rising INDPRO during early-cycle phases, or the signal reverses direction during late-cycle phases without regime adjustment.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate expanding construction activity and rising household wealth effects, which mechanically increase equity market liquidity and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows, or housing starts growth fails to predict positive returns in > 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 240.0, it confirms entrenched inflation that triggers aggressive central bank policy normalization, compressing equity multiples and yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 240.0, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for Fed forward guidance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate signals a robust labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative returns in > 50% of observations, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly GDP growth outpaces industrial production expansion, it indicates demand-supply imbalances that strain capacity and elevate inflation risks, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of quarters where GDP growth exceeds industrial production growth, or the signal fails to predict returns during supply-constrained regimes.
